name: Soutelo de Montes - circular through Sierra de Cando
name_original: Soutelo de Montes - circular por Sierra de Cando
region_intro: Mountain biking around Forcarei offers diverse terrain within the interior of Pontevedra province, Galicia. The region is characterized by mountains, lush forests, and rivers, providing a natural setting for outdoor activities. The Serra do Candán, a significant natural feature, contributes to the area's ecological value with its rich flora and fauna. Mountain bikers can explore routes through native oak and chestnut trees, encountering varied elevations. Best mountain bike trails around Forcarei The most popular mountain bike trail is Soutelo de Montes - circular through Sierra de Cando, a 31.0 miles (50.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 50 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends through the Sierra de Cando, offering significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Carballedo River Beach – Roman road of Carballedo loop from Cotobade, a moderate 17.2 miles (27.7 km) path. This route explores a river beach and follows sections of an ancient Roman road. Local mountain bikers also love the Carboeiro Monastery – Monastery of Carboeiro loop from A Moa, a 19.6 miles (31.6 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours. Mountain biking around Forcarei is defined by mountainous terrain, lush forests, and river valleys, with routes often passing through areas of native oak and chestnut trees. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging climbs. The routes in Forcarei are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 2 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Forcarei's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Forcarei?
A: Forcarei offers a selection of 9 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. The region is known for its diverse terrain, including mountainous areas, lush forests, and river valleys.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Forcarei?
A: Mountain biking in Forcarei features varied terrain, from moderate paths to challenging climbs. You'll often find yourself riding through areas of native oak and chestnut trees, with routes ascending through mountain ranges like the Serra do Candán. Some more difficult sections may even require dismounting and pushing your bike.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Forcarei?
A: While there are no trails specifically graded as 'easy' in our current selection, 7 of the 9 routes are classified as moderate. These moderate trails offer a good balance for riders with some experience, allowing them to enjoy the region's natural beauty without extreme technical demands.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes in Forcarei?
A: For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983410395/soutelo-de-montes-circular-through-sierra-de-cando" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soutelo de Montes - circular through Sierra de Cando&lt;/a&gt; route is a difficult 31.0-mile (50.0 km) trail with significant elevation gain, taking approximately 4 hours 50 minutes to complete. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico do Candán loop from Millarada&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate route that still offers a substantial climb to the summit.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by mountain bike in Forcarei?
A: Yes, Forcarei is home to several stunning waterfalls. The Fervenza do Toxa is Galicia's highest free-fall waterfall, plunging over 60 meters. Other notable cascades include the Fervenza de Escuadro and the charming Molino y Fervenza de Quintillán, which features an old mill. While not all are directly on MTB trails, many are within easy reach of the general riding area.

Q: Where can I find the best scenic views while mountain biking in Forcarei?
A: For breathtaking vistas, consider routes that ascend to higher elevations. The Serra do Candán offers spectacular views from points like Monte Coco. Panoramic viewpoints such as Miradoiro das Neveiras de Fixó, Alto do Seixo, Alto do Candán, and Pico do Costoia provide expansive views of the surrounding 'Terra de Montes' region.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore along the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, you can discover several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1773315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico do Candán&lt;/a&gt; summit offers panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6287147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carballedo River Beach&lt;/a&gt; provides a scenic spot to relax. The region is also rich in lush forests, primarily native oak and chestnut trees, creating a 'green paradise' for nature lovers.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural landmarks to visit while mountain biking in Forcarei?
A: Yes, Forcarei offers cultural and historical points of interest. The 12th-century Cistercian &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Santa María de Aciveiro&lt;/a&gt; is a significant historical treasure. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3854687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taboada Medieval Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, a notable site that was once part of a royal road. The Observatorio Astronómico de Forcarei is another unique attraction for stargazing.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Forcarei?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Forcarei are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983410395/soutelo-de-montes-circular-through-sierra-de-cando" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soutelo de Montes - circular through Sierra de Cando&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40308865" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Pozo do Boi River Beach loop from Currospedriños&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Forcarei?
A: The mountain bike trails in Forcarei are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the region's diverse terrain, the beauty of its lush forests, and the authentic rural Galician experience it provides, far from mass tourism.

Q: Is Forcarei a good destination for family-friendly mountain biking?
A: Forcarei offers moderate trails that can be suitable for families with some mountain biking experience. While there are no specific 'easy' graded trails, the moderate routes provide an opportunity to enjoy the natural environment together. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Are mountain bike trails in Forcarei dog-friendly?
A: Many outdoor trails in Galicia, including those in Forcarei, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check specific trail regulations or local signage. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the chosen route and carry enough water for both of you.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Forcarei?
A: Forcarei's abundant rainfall contributes to its lush landscapes, making it green year-round. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, with less heat than summer and potentially drier conditions than winter. However, the region's natural beauty can be enjoyed in any season, with winter offering a unique, serene experience.
tips: Mountain trails up to the Alto de Paraño summit 800m above sea level with wind farm along the ridge. Spectacular views between the province of Ourense and Pontevedra
Impressive place to rest, make a stop along the way, THERE IS NO BAR OR CHIRINGITO, you have to bring food and drink, if there are toilets, the place is amazing and the water is very, very cold.

Trails through the wild surroundings of Grose and Blackberry leading to the top of the mountain at an altitude of over 1000 m will reach the wind farm facing the Sierra Condan massif. Spectacular views on clear days.

waypoints.name: O Pozo do Boi River Beach
